original_text:
### Typography Hierarchy (fonts, weights, cases)
| Level | Print | Digital / Email | Word / PPT / Excel fallback |
|---|---|---|---|
| Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos** |
| Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos** |
| Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos |
 
- Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
- Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
- Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.
- **[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`. Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.
- **[GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height):**
  - H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
  - H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
  - H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
  - Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
  - Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content
  - Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%) — must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
  - Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)
- **Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).
- Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

quotes: Email font stack (CSS): 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only
Nunito Sans (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case

Email font stack (CSS): 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif. Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook

rules.intent: Maintain cross-channel typographical brand consistency while guaranteeing graceful fallbacks in webfont-restricted email clients.
rules.rule_text: Digital and email layouts must load and apply Nunito Sans as the primary typeface using the fallback stack 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif to handle environments that strip webfonts. Agenda is reserved for print, but may be used as live text in digital platforms specifically for campaign headlines only. Aptos serves as the default fallback for Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, and Excel.

quotes: 16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)
Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Maintain layout accessibility and clean readability through an ordered, non-ambiguous visual type scale.
rules.rule_text: Layouts must strictly follow the established email type scale and visual hierarchy: H1 hero headlines at 28px (type_scale.h1), H2 headings at 22px (type_scale.h2), H3 subheads at 18px (type_scale.h3), and standard body copy at a brand-mandated minimum of 16px (type_scale.body). Secondary supporting copy may be 14px (type_scale.secondary_body), and footnote/reference copy must be 12px (type_scale.footnote) and visually distinct. Running body copy must never fall below the absolute minimum of 12px. Distinct, adjacent text blocks must not share identical sizing and weights if they perform different roles in the visual hierarchy.

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy (brand accessibility rule). For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further — use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Enhance text readability and reach brand compliance accessibility standards across both light and dark layouts.
rules.rule_text: To guarantee accessibility, body copy must use a minimum line height of 150% (line_height.accessibility.body_min = 150%). For light colored text on dark backgrounds, line height must be increased further to a minimum of 160% (e.g., 16px text / 26px line height).
